data.title: North Korean hackers use fake job offers to steal crypto, with victims now wary of connections. Analyst warns of increased sophistication.
data.description: A recent report reveals that North Korean cybercriminals are resorting to fake job offers to gain access to the cryptocurrency industry and steal digital currency. This issue has escalated to the extent that job seekers are now cautious of recruiters with potential North Korean ties. According to experts, victims, and corporate representatives cited by Reuters, individuals must be wary of job offers. Carlos Yanez, a business development executive at Global Ledger, a blockchain analytics company in Switzerland, was targeted by these cybercriminals. Yanez noted a significant advancement in the North Korean hackers' tactics in the past year, describing their sophistication as alarming. The threats posed by these cyberattacks are concerning, as evident in Yanez's experience. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Lawyer Mark Zuckerberg sues Meta CEO for disabling his Facebook page 5 times in 8 years. He spent $11,000 on Meta ads.
data.description: An attorney named Mark Zuckerberg based in Indiana has brought a legal action against Mark Zuckerberg, the CEO of Meta Platforms Inc. (NASDAQ: META), due to repeated suspensions of his Facebook page. The lawyer Mark Zuckerberg utilized his Facebook page for business purposes but encountered five instances of page suspension in the past eight years. As per TechCrunch's report, Meta's moderation system flagged his account as falsely posing as the platform's founder, Mark Zuckerberg. The lawyer, who insists he is the authentic Mark Zuckerberg, has invested over $11,000 on Meta platforms for page promotions. Despite spending on advertisements, his account faced continuous...
